channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: I don't know what a flesh disk jukebox is, but the Lynchian adventure game Something Beautiful makes me want to find out
item.content:encoded:
I want to see something beautiful. Enter stage left Something Beautiful, a "music-driven" point-and-click adventure game that has the vibe of a cursed CD-ROM you find in your parents storage unit where you look for answers in a strange, surreal, unsettling world filled with fleshy, anthropomorphic puzzles.

Something Beautiful comes from artist and musician Murlo, whose auditory stylings range from alien bar lounge to PS1 platformer you've never heard of in vibe. It is, as far as I can tell, their first game, and truthfully it's a struggle to truly capture, or explain, this game in words alone (in part because the game's Steam page is light on concrete details). The game opens with you, a fairly regular looking guy, waking up in a "socially fractured industrial estate," where there do appear to be other regular looking guys, as well as puzzles made up of humanoid creatures that look like God's initial attempt at making us.

These creatures themselves in part appear to be the puzzles you will try to solve. One type of puzzle appears to show pucks emanating from the body of one of these humanoids, with you having to stamp them down at the right time. Another shows parts of a leg glowing. I don't know what you're meant to do with this one. Other sections of the game see you looking at books that even appear to directly reference Murlo's own discography. I think you can even play a man at some points. It is a disorienting game to look at, but I am quite enamoured by it for as little as can be seen of it.

Murlo even promises the embedding of a whole album within the game world itself, "from interactive music puzzles to surreal 'Flesh Disk' jukeboxes." Nightmarish, certainly, Lynchian, most definitely. No release date attached to Something Beautiful as it stands, but you can wishlist it on Steam while you wait for one.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/i-dont-know-what-a-flesh-disk-jukebox-is-but-the-lynchian-adventure-game-something-beautiful-makes-me-want-to-find-out
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Enshrouded makes October even busier with a 1.0 release date, more details coming this summer
item.content:encoded:
Unshrouded? Is that anything?

There comes a time in the life of most early access releases that they most abandon that moniker in favour of a fully fledged 1.0 launch. And it appears said time is arriving for Enshrouded, the fantasy survival game that comes with a whole lot of building options, as developer Keen Games GmbH have locked in a 1.0 release date.

As everyone under the sun is doing, Enshrouded is avoiding the impending nuclear warhead that is Grand Theft Auto 6's November launch. Instead, it's opting for an October 15th release date, leaving it plenty of room after the fact. What exactly is coming with that 1.0 launch hasn't been shared just yet. Further details will apparently be coming sometime this summer, but other than that the only hint appears to be a teasing shot in the announcement trailer of some kind of big ole, I want to say, tree? Beckoning you to "reach for the skies."

When Enshrouded does finally leave early access in October, it'll have been almost three years since it first launched. The game pits you as a Flameborn, the "last ember of hope of a dying race." In practice, you venture throughout a fantasy world taking on some boss fights, trying out some dungeons, and building some bits yourself.

Speaking of, earlier this year Enshrouded introduced its Adventure Sharing mode in an update that furthers all games' Robloxification. This mode essentially lets you, as you may have guessed from the name, share your creations with others. Given that you can make your own dungeons, obstacle courses, or even just castles, this is very much one of those UGC type dealios to try and bring more people in. Adventure Sharing isn't fully ready quite yet, but perhaps by 1.0 it'll be ready for you to dive into fully.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/enshrouded-makes-october-even-busier-with-a-10-release-date-more-details-coming-this-summer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
